skill.name: similarity-search-patterns
skill.description: Design and implement similarity search systems with vector databases, hybrid retrieval, indexing strategies, and performance tuning patterns.
skill.summary: Build vector search, semantic retrieval, and hybrid search with production-oriented database patterns.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged code execution, environment access, weak cryptography, network activity, and a critical combined-behavior heuristic. Review found the shell, credential, weak-crypto, and reconnaissance findings are false positives from Markdown formatting, parameter names, SQL text, and documentation links. One real medium-risk issue remains: a sample pgvector query interpolates metadata keys into SQL and should require allowlisted filter fields.

medium_findings.title: SQL Metadata Key Interpolation in Sample Code
medium_findings.description: The pgvector search example parameterizes values but interpolates metadata field names into a SQL expression. If callers pass untrusted filter keys, copied implementations could allow SQL injection or query errors. Use an allowlist of metadata keys before constructing this clause.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code clearly interpolates filter keys into SQL while only parameterizing values. The risk depends on whether application callers can control metadata keys.

low_findings.title: Markdown Code Fences Misidentified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The external command findings are false positives. The flagged backticks are Markdown code fences and inline resource references, not executable Ruby or shell code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Each flagged location is Markdown syntax, not runtime code. No command interpreter or user-controlled command execution path is present.

low_findings.title: API Key Parameter Misidentified as Secret Access
low_findings.description: The environment access findings are false positives. The Pinecone example accepts an API key parameter and passes it to the Pinecone client; it does not read environment variables or expose a hardcoded secret.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence shows a typed constructor parameter and client initialization. No process environment access, secret logging, or outbound exfiltration endpoint is shown.

low_findings.title: Network Findings Are Expected Database and Documentation References
low_findings.description: The network findings do not show hidden exfiltration. The fetch calls are asyncpg database queries, the URL is a localhost Weaviate endpoint, and the remaining URLs are vendor documentation links.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines are ordinary vector database examples and reference links. They do not send secrets or local data to an unexpected domain.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Findings Are Unsupported
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography findings are false positives. The flagged SKILL.md line is descriptive text, and the flagged playbook line is SQL ordering by a score.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Neither location contains MD5, SHA-1, encryption, or password hashing logic. The static signal does not match the file contents.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Findings Are Benign Text or Query Code
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance findings are false positives. The flagged lines are comments, SQL table fields, search method documentation, join syntax, and best-practice text.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are part of similarity search examples and prose. They do not inspect host state, enumerate files, or gather system information.

dangerous_patterns.title: Unvalidated SQL Identifier Construction
dangerous_patterns.description: The sample builds part of a SQL predicate from metadata keys. This pattern is dangerous when keys come from users; implementations should map requested filters to known column or JSON key names.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The code uses parameter binding for values but not for the metadata key identifier. The issue is real for untrusted filter keys, but legitimate use is possible with internal allowlists.

content.value_statement: Teams often struggle to choose the right vector database pattern for semantic search. This skill provides practical templates for Pinecone, Qdrant, pgvector, Weaviate, hybrid retrieval, and tuning.
actual_capabilities: Explains distance metrics for cosine, Euclidean, dot product, and Manhattan search.
Provides implementation patterns for Pinecone, Qdrant, pgvector, and Weaviate.
Shows batch upsert, vector search, metadata filtering, and deletion workflows.
Covers reranking and hybrid search with vector and keyword signals.
Lists tuning practices for index type, recall, latency, and cost.
limitations: It provides templates and guidance, not a complete running application.
Examples need adaptation for authentication, schema design, and deployment settings.
The pgvector filter example should use allowlisted metadata keys before production use.
It does not benchmark your data or choose parameters automatically.

use_cases.title: Plan a RAG Retrieval Layer
use_cases.description: Select a vector store pattern and retrieval flow for a document question-answering system.

use_cases.title: Add Hybrid Product Search
use_cases.description: Combine semantic vectors with keyword matching to improve search precision and recall.

use_cases.title: Tune Vector Database Performance
use_cases.description: Review index choices, batch sizes, filters, and recall settings for a production workload.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a Search Pattern
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me choose a similarity search pattern for my app. I have this data size, latency target, embedding model, and filter requirements: [details].

prompt_templates.title: Design a Vector Store
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a vector store implementation for [Pinecone, Qdrant, pgvector, or Weaviate]. Include upsert, search, metadata filtering, and deletion steps.

prompt_templates.title: Improve Hybrid Retrieval
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my semantic search flow and propose a hybrid retrieval plan. Consider reranking, keyword weighting, metadata filters, and recall measurement.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Production Readiness
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this similarity search design for production readiness. Check scalability, latency, cost, schema safety, index parameters, and observability gaps.

output_examples.input: I need semantic search for 500,000 support articles with metadata filters.
output_examples.output: A recommended HNSW-based vector index, a metadata filter strategy, batching guidance, and a rollout checklist for recall testing.

output_examples.input: My vector search misses exact product names and part numbers.
output_examples.output: A hybrid search plan that combines BM25 keyword matching with vector similarity, then reranks the combined candidate set.

output_examples.input: Our pgvector search is slow after adding more documents.
output_examples.output: A focused tuning checklist for index selection, query limits, pre-filtering, connection pooling, and recall-latency measurement.

best_practices: Start with clear recall and latency targets before choosing an index type.
Use hybrid retrieval when exact terms, identifiers, or rare keywords matter.
Validate metadata filter keys with an allowlist before building database queries.
anti_patterns: Do not copy sample database code into production without authentication and input validation.
Do not optimize index parameters before measuring recall on representative queries.
Do not rely only on vector similarity when keyword precision is a core requirement.

faq.question: Which vector databases does this skill cover?
faq.answer: It covers Pinecone, Qdrant, pgvector, and Weaviate implementation patterns.

faq.question: Can it help with RAG retrieval?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes retrieval, reranking, filtering, and hybrid search patterns useful for RAG systems.

faq.question: Does it run a vector database for me?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance and templates that you adapt to your own infrastructure.

faq.question: Does it include production safety guidance?
faq.answer: Partly. It covers performance practices, but applications must add authentication, validation, monitoring, and deployment controls.

faq.question: Why is the security risk marked medium?
faq.answer: One pgvector example builds a SQL predicate from metadata keys. Production code should allowlist those keys.

faq.question: Can I use it with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The marketplace metadata lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
